assertions.text: I was making a right turn onto the southbound road before being struck.
Another car struck the left side of my Outback.
I had just accelerated from a near stop (my vehicle was starting from near-zero speed and increasing).
I was not braking at the time; I had just accelerated from a near stop (implies no braking immediately before impact).
There was one impact (another car struck my Outback once).
The collision occurred while I was turning (the impact occurred during the right-turn maneuver).

verdicts.evidence: The telemetry shows steering angle changed from [172.5 deg baseline_median with a peak_raw_value of 17.5 deg at t=-0.5 s](T4) and the steering channel samples include values [172.5, 137.5, 105.0, 45.0, 17.5, 22.5] over t in [-5,0] (T2), but device-frame steering and mapping to a 'right turn onto the southbound road' (direction, road heading) is not determined by sensors alone, so whether the maneuver was a right turn onto the southbound road is unverifiable.

The crash delta-V shows a lateral change peaking at [4.0 km/h at t=0.07 s](T1) with a final lateral delta-V of [2.0 km/h](T4) and a longitudinal negative delta-V of [−17.0 km/h](T4), which indicates a significant forward deceleration and some lateral impulse, but the sensors are in device axes and no mapping to vehicle left/right is provided; thus 'struck the left side' is unverifiable from these channels alone.

Speed rose from [6.0 km/h at t=-5.0 s] to [28.0 km/h at t=0.0 s] in the pre-crash series (change of [22.0 km/h]) (T2)(T4), and accelerator pedal showed increases up to [22.0% at t=-1.0 s] before dropping to [0.0% at t=-0.5 s and 0.0% at t=0.0 s] (T2), consistent with the vehicle starting from near-zero and accelerating prior to impact.

The brake channel shows an off→on transition at [t=-0.5 s] with the last two pre-crash samples at [1.0 at t=-0.5 s] and [1.0 at t=0.0 s] (T2) and compute_stats reports a transition at [t=-0.5 s] and fraction_on [0.18] (T4), so the vehicle was braking immediately before impact which contradicts the claim of no braking.

EDR summary and crash-pulse data indicate a single crash record with cumulative longitudinal delta-V reaching [−17.0 km/h at t=0.19 s] and no multiple impact events reported; compute_stats shows n_samples for delta-V consistent with one pulse and event_desc 'Record 1' (T1)(T4)(T3).

Steering input changed substantially before impact (steering samples show values moving toward [17.5 deg at t=-0.5 s] from higher values earlier; T2) and speed was changing (T2), but sensors do not by themselves establish that the impact occurred during the right-turn maneuver onto a specific southbound road; therefore timing of impact relative to 'the turning maneuver' is unverifiable.

result.rationale: Telemetry confirms the vehicle was accelerating from low speed ([6.0→28.0 km/h], T2/T4), and a crash pulse with [−17.0 km/h longitudinal delta-V] occurred (T3/T4). Brake channel shows an application at [t=-0.5 s] (T2/T4), and lateral delta-V is small ([4.0 km/h peak], T3/T4) but device-axis/left-right mapping and road heading are not available, making direction and road-heading assertions unverifiable.

citations.claim: 172.5 deg baseline_median with a peak_raw_value of 17.5 deg at t=-0.5 s

result.time_zero: impact (t=0); pre-crash negative, crash pulse positive

channels.name: delta_v_long
channels.desc: Cumulative longitudinal delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

channels.name: delta_v_lat
channels.desc: Cumulative lateral delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

result.notes: NHTSA CISS EDR data: sparse pre-crash series (~2 Hz, t<0) plus a crash-pulse delta-V curve (t>=0, ~10 ms steps). t=0 is the impact.

result.downsampling: per-bucket sample with the largest |deviation from channel median| is kept
per-bucket sample with the largest |deviation from channel median| is kept

delta_v_long.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse
